reviews.review_description:
This game is super light on content which explains the $6 price tag. It’s basically realistic graphics overlaid with this game along with a real human bowler. 
These are fantastic concepts but ultimately it’s just an open area practice game. 
The gameplay itself is decent and has bowlers of a few types and you can adjust a few knobs but there are no fielders, no scores, no stadiums, no modes, no white ball only red, no day night cycle or night etc. 
it’s basically you and a bowler in an open field. The gameplay itself was fairly decent but needs some improvement for batting. I could bat and hit here and there but that’s about it. The spin bowling is odd and goofy given the ball flies two storeys high for every single one. Hitting bowlers instead that easy and then you can hit A or B to see the helmet or ball trace fully which is a nice touch. 

Overall I think with money the dev team might put something of a more finished product soon as it has good potential.

developer_response.body:
Hi, 
Based on your feedback, there has been improvements to the game.
Could you be kind enough to review it again.

reviews.review_description: This cricket batting simulator is as close as you can get to action, without stepping into a cricket ground.

reviews.review_title: Very nice for game in development
reviews.review_description: Very impressed with the game. Little game options but really love the real life ground and real bowler bowling at you. Great physics. If more game modes are added then this will beat all vr cricket games.
